doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having this dry cough for about a week and a half now. It’s, like, intermittent and lasts just a few seconds each time. I’m kinda worried because it’s been persistent, you know?

doctor_turn: Has the cough changed in severity or frequency since it first started?
patient_turn: Um, well, I think it started about a week and a half ago, maybe a little longer. You know, I was actually watching a movie when I first noticed it, and I remember thinking it was just a little tickle in my throat. Oh, and during that time, I also made some popcorn, which was really good, but I think it might have made me cough more!

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like when it happens?

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like when it happens? Is it dry, or do you feel any irritation?

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the cough starting?

doctor_turn: Has the cough changed in severity or frequency since it first started?

doctor_turn: Did your cough ever produce any sputum, and if so, what did it look like?
patient_turn: Yeah, at first it did produce some sputum, but it was just watery and, um, there was no blood or anything like that. But now, I don’t have any sputum production at all, it’s just dry.

doctor_turn: Does smoking make your cough feel worse when you smoke?
patient_turn: Um, yeah, it definitely seems to make it worse when I smoke. I’ve noticed that I cough more after having a cigarette, which is, like, kinda frustrating since I’m trying to quit. It’s just, you know, an annoying cycle.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any shortness of breath along with your cough?

patient_turn: No, I haven’t felt any chest pain. It’s just the cough and, um, some irritation in my throat, but no pain or anything like that, which is good, I guess.
